question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: toNodeProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a networkNode response with an empty or null certHash (Strings.CS.remove returns empty string) so that toNodeProperties() creates NodeProperties with no certificate validation, allowing the attacker to impersonate a legitimate node and receive operator-signed transactions without the operator's approval for that node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the secureRandom.nextInt(publishProperties.getClients()) client selection in publish() to predict or influence which pre-authorized client handles a transaction, enabling targeted attacks on specific client instances that may have weaker key protection?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: next()] Can an unprivileged external user set receiptPercent=1.0 and recordPercent=1.0 in PublishScenarioProperties to force next() to generate PublishRequests with both receipt and record queries, causing the operator's key to authorize additional query payments without per-query owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger processTransactionResponse() with sendRecord=true to execute a TransactionRecordQuery with a hardcoded 1 HBAR payment signed by the operator, draining operator funds through repeated record queries without the operator's per-query approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: convert()] Can an unprivileged external user inject an expression in the properties map processed by expressionConverter.convert() that resolves to an attacker-controlled account ID, causing the TransactionSupplier to generate transactions targeting that account and signed by the operator without the target account owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @ConfigurationPropertiesScan annotation activated by SpringApplication.run() to inject malicious configuration properties via environment variables or system properties that override the operator's private key, causing transactions to be signed with an attacker-controlled key without the real owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Mono.fromFuture() async execution path in execute() to submit a transaction asynchronously and then cancel the future before receipt processing, leaving a signed transaction on the network that was authorized without the owner's knowledge of the final outcome?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a PublishRequest with a pre-built transaction that already has node account IDs set, bypassing the nodeSupplier.get() call in getTransactionResponse() and routing the operator-signed transaction to an attacker-controlled node without the operator's approval for that routing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: next()] Can an unprivileged external user set tps to an extremely high value in PublishScenarioProperties to cause next() to generate thousands of transactions per second signed by the operator's key, effectively authorizing a flood of transactions without the operator's per-transaction approval?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: next()] Can an unprivileged external user set limit to Long.MAX_VALUE (via getLimit() returning Long.MAX_VALUE when limit<=0) in PublishScenarioProperties to cause next() to generate an unlimited number of operator-signed transactions without any owner approval gate?
